prose:
Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) energy gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF v? (via chemkit)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied aniline coordinates at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz — no re-optimization performed (reported as DFT//input-XYZ)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence: achieved in 9 cycles; scf_converged = true
Warnings: none reported by the engine
Live log: a real-time SCF/analysis transcript was streamed to frontier_<timestamp>.out (created automatically in the run directory); the full result JSON is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmppmhekxy_/live.json

Result
HOMO energy = −5.553772 eV
LUMO energy = −0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV (Koopmans’/orbital gap, not an excited-state energy)

The calculation was carried out with chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill using the command recorded in the JSON’s cli_invocation field:
/…/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 … aniline.xyz
No engine warnings were issued and the integrity check reports status = ok, trustworthy = true, so the value above is reported as computed and trustworthy.
